behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/segmentation logic at a single code boundary. No real infra, subprocess, or cross-module handoff is required to reveal the failure for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Add a focused parser test that feeds a multiline shell snippet with newline-separated assignments and a command, then asserts the segment list contains distinct statements in order (not one combined block).

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: The incident was caused by module interaction (segmentation + trigger classification) where the whole block became `not_a_trigger`. Unit-only tests on either module can miss this handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse classification pipeline end-to-end in-process with a multiline command block (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`) and assert the final classification is PR-create trigger, not `not_a_trigger`.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the same cross-module path as behavior 2, but for negative discrimination. It validates segmentation + classifier interaction and guards against over-triggering.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process PostToolUse pipeline with multiline assignments ending in `gh issue create ...`; assert classification is not PR-create trigger (and remains issue/non-trigger per spec).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: This depends on wiring between detection, PR-id reconstruction, and state persistence (local filesystem/module boundary). No explicit requirement for real external API/subprocess contract checks in this behavior text, so System is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp workspace, drive PostToolUse with a detected PR-create event containing representative command/output context, then assert persisted review-loop state includes the reconstructed PR identifier with expected schema fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require validating real gh/network/subprocess contracts; local in-process round-trip of persistence is sufficient for this behavior.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is an inter-module compatibility check (writer hook -> state artifact -> reader/enforcer hook). It needs a real handoff through persisted state, but not real external HTTP/LLM/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run PostToolUse to write gate state, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against the same state location and assert enforcement decision reflects that PR review loop is active (i.e., readback/enforcement succeeds).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System was considered due round-trip side effect language, but rejected because the behavior specifies local hook-to-hook state interoperability, not an external service/API contract.
